threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you’re new if you still call it a 'cafe' instead of a 'caff' and look confused when someone orders a bacon sarnie.
Saw a bloke on the 188 serenading a stray cat with opera. The cat just sat there, unimpressed, like it could hear better in the daylight.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an old man feeding pigeons while a street artist painted a mural right beside him. Berlin's chaos feels cozy sometimes.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see guys creating art from discarded bus stops. It's like a pop-up gallery on the sidewalk, each piece telling a lost story.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their calm acceptance of Muni delays caused by random dogs—it's a daily part of life here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton waiting 20 minutes while a dog lounged on the tracks. The whole bus was just chuckling at the epic standoff.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best food is at the flashy places. Try the little taqueria on Carrer de Providència - they don’t even have a menu.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confusion over the 'no smoking' signs at popular plazas—most locals just light up anyway.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Clissold Park squirrels' are cute instead of just annoyingly bold at lunchtime.
Last night on the 343, a guy serenaded us with an out-of-tune guitar. Most just stared, but the woman next to him was totally vibing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? When I saw a group of kids playing Fußball in the street, dodging the construction cones like it was a World Cup final.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a party that starts in a U-Bahn car and ends with street food at 3 AM. It’s a wild ride, literally!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows not to bother with the 14 bus when BART's down—I've seen the same folks waiting hours, splashing in puddles like it's normal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 19 bus serenading everyone with a ukulele, barely noticed the delays. Only in SF do the Muni blues become a mini concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every restaurant serves paella; most locals eat it at home or on special occasions, not on every corner.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their footwear; good luck hiking those cobblestones in those pristine white sneakers.
